content: Multi-writer artifacts with 'create-once' write protocols are a recurring source of semantic gaps in creative-production systems. When multiple specialist agents write to the same artifact (e.g., characters/index.json written by protagonist-profiler and supporting-cast-developer), the protocol must be 'read-modify-write' with additive semantics, not 'create-once'. This was flagged as warnings AF-ARTIFACT-04a/04b/04c in the audit — 3 artifacts had create-once protocols despite having 2-3 writers. Future runs should default multi-writer artifacts to read-modify-write and require explicit justification for create-once.
sourceEvidence: AF-ARTIFACT-04a: 'characters/index.json is declared writeProtocol=create-once but has 2 writers... If protagonist-profiler writes first, supporting-cast-developer would see the file exists and incorrectly think it is in a retry.' AF-ARTIFACT-04b: 'characters/{CHAR-NNN}.json... has 3 writers... character-voice-designer needs to add voice fingerprints to existing character files — this is semantically a read-modify-write operation.'

history.reason: Cold start — first observation from initial factory run
